title: Nyctocereus neumannii
intro: Nyctocereus neumannii, also known as Neumann's night-blooming cereus, is a species of cactus native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its white, funnel-shaped flowers that bloom at night. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Nyctocereus neumannii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat colds, coughs, and respiratory infections, as well as to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
taxonomy: Nyctocereus neumannii belongs to the family Cactaceae. The genus Nyctocereus is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Americas. Specifically, Nyctocereus neumannii is classified as a species within the genus Nyctocereus. Nyctocereus neumannii is a type of columnar cactus. It is a part of the Cactaceae family along with other species like Cereus and Stetsonia.
